name: Screen job applicants with PostgreSQL and Twilio on autopilot

parameters.formTitle: Screen job applicants with PostgreSQL and Twilio on autopilo

parameters.message: =Thanks for your interest — here's a quick-start guide.

parameters.query: SELECT * FROM candidates WHERE updated_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day' ORDER BY updated_at DESC;

parameters.message: =✅ Screen job applicants with PostgreSQL and Twilio on autopilot — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### New Candidate Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Role, Stage). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Keep Valid Candidates

### Keep Valid Candidates

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Score the Lead

### Score the Lead

Custom JavaScript: Score each candidate 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Assign Round-Robin Owner

### Assign Round-Robin Owner

Custom JavaScript: Round-robin owner assignment

Next: Route by Heat

### Route by Heat

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: hot, warm + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Hot → PostgreSQL + Instant Ping · ②→ Warm → Drip via Twilio · ③→ Cold → Park in Nurture List

### Hot → PostgreSQL + Instant Ping

Runs a parameterized `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT` upsert — parameters are bound via `queryReplacement`, never string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Notify Assigned Owner · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Notify Assigned Owner

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Ports: ①→ Combine Lead Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Warm → Drip via Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Ports: ①→ Combine Lead Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Cold → Park in Nurture List

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Combine Lead Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

nodes.name: Note: Cold → Park in Nurture List

parameters.content:
### Combine Lead Paths

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per candidate (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Candidate Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Twilio · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Twilio

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#hr`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Candidate Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Screen job applicants with PostgreSQL and Twilio on autopilot

**Pattern:** Recruitment Pipeline · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Screen job applicants with PostgreSQL and Twilio on autopilot. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual onboarding is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Candidate Form Submission → Keep Valid Candidates → Score the Lead → Assign Round-Robin Owner → Route by Heat → Hot → PostgreSQL + Instant Ping → Notify Assigned Owner → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to recruitment pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating onboarding in human resources
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Round-robin owner assignment happens right after scoring, before routing — every lead gets an accountable owner regardless of tier, so even a 'cold' lead parked in the nurture list is someone's responsibility later, not an anonymous row in a spreadsheet.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Change the hot/warm threshold values and watch the tier distribution shift.
2. Add a 4th owner to the round-robin pool.
3. Add a follow-up wait+check to the warm-drip branch, similar to the drip-ladder spine.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your onboarding process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
